fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Pennsylvania Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Pennsylvania - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity pennsylvania, how to transfer physical therapist license to pennsylvania, physical therapist compact pennsylvania, pennsylvania physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Pennsylvania Board of Physical Therapy
board.mailing_address:
State Board of Physical Therapy
P.O. Box 2649
Harrisburg, PA 17105-2649

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Pennsylvania.

steps.title: Create a PALS Account
steps.description: Go to the Pennsylvania Licensing System (PALS) portal at https://www.pals.pa.gov/ and register a new user account if you do not already have one, or log in to your existing account.

steps.title: Start New Application
steps.description: Once logged into your PALS account, click on 'New Application', then select the 'Physical Therapist' license type. Choose the appropriate application route, such as 'Initial Licensure by Examination' or 'Initial Licensure by Endorsement'.

steps.title: Complete Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form within PALS, upload any necessary documents electronically, and pay the non-refundable application fee using a credit or debit card.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your physical therapy program to be sent directly to the Board. If applying by examination, ensure your National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) scores are submitted. If applying by endorsement, provide verification of licensure from any other state(s) where you hold or have held a license, sent directly from the issuing board.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprint-Based Background Check
steps.description: Register for and complete a fingerprint-based criminal background check through the designated vendor, IdentoGO. Follow the specific instructions provided during the PALS application process to ensure your results are sent to the Pennsylvania State Board of Physical Therapy.

steps.title: Board Review and Approval
steps.description: The Pennsylvania State Board of Physical Therapy will review your complete application and all submitted supporting documentation. Upon successful review and approval, your Physical Therapist license will be issued and viewable within your PALS account.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Pennsylvania physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $45. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Pennsylvania have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Pennsylvania participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Pennsylvania without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 2 to 3 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Pennsylvania while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Pennsylvania
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Pennsylvania, you must use the Pennsylvania Licensing System (PALS) online portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Navigate to the PALS portal at https://www.pals.pa.gov/. 
2. Click 'Register New Account' if you are a new user, or 'Login' if you already have an account. 
3. Follow the prompts to create your account by providing personal information and setting up your username and password. 
4. Once logged in, click 'New Application' to begin the licensing process.
